segments.title: Core reference books for Blackburn aircraft
segments.summary: Dr. Clarke points to aircraft-specification and Blackburn history books as starting references for tracing the company's designs.
segments.body: This short guide identifies the printed works behind the Blackburn series, including a British aircraft-specification reference and A. J. Jackson's Blackburn Aircraft since 1909. The value is not simply a list of titles: specification data and a focused company history help distinguish a design's technical paper trail from its wider production story. It is a useful entry point for anyone following Blackburn aircraft across the interwar naval-aviation period.

evidence.note: The transcript names a British aircraft-specification volume and A. J. Jackson's Blackburn Aircraft since 1909 as useful sources.

segments.title: Why Blackburn's period aircraft names need checking
segments.summary: A history website helps reconcile older Blackburn aircraft names and variant labels with the names used by modern institutions.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke recommends the British Aerospace history website when a historic aircraft name does not match the modern name a reader encounters elsewhere. He explains that period names, later dominant labels, and variant names can pull in different directions. That matters especially for his work on aircraft carriers and naval aviation in the 1920s and 1930s, where following the original terminology is essential for connecting sources accurately.

evidence.note: The transcript recommends a British Aerospace history site and explains the mismatch between period aircraft names, variants, and later naming.
